definition: Relative forecast performance of analysts versus an AI ensemble and a hybrid model

definition: Occupation-level fintech patent exposure and worker and firm outcomes
unit: basis points per exposure percentile; percentage points
coverage: United States; mainly 2010-2018 postings and employment; published JFE 2025 specifications
notes: Published JFE estimates supersede working-paper magnitudes; postings are vacancy flows rather than layoffs or employment stocks; exposure predicts outcomes but is not observed adoption or a clean causal experiment; inventor gains are relative interactions and acquisition-only interactions are insignificant

definition: Supervisory approaches to data risks in AI use by financial institutions

definition: Annual share of AI-assisted analyst recommendations more accurate than AI-only recommendations
notes: Original working-paper visual; the hybrid combines the authors' AI forecast with recent analyst consensus information.
crop_scope: Unredrawn crop from PDF page 35 at 300 dpi: x=175 y=350 width=2200 height=1800 pixels; retains figure title, explanatory note, axes, 95% confidence interval, 50% reference, trend line, and legend.
interpretation_boundary: Supports complementarity in this forecast task and sample only; it does not establish that hybrid systems dominate in every financial task or with current AI systems.

definition: Relation between occupation-level fintech exposure percentile and cumulative change in job-posting share from 2007 to 2018
coverage: United States; Burning Glass Technologies job postings; 2007-2018

interpretation_boundary: Supports an exposure-related reallocation of vacancy shares; postings are flows rather than employment stocks or layoffs, and the curve is not a clean causal estimate of adoption.
